title: 2 Samuel: Sin and Consequences
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of 2 samuel: sin and consequences and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study 2 samuel: sin and consequences and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: What prophet confronted David over his sin with Bathsheba and murder of Uriah? Answer: Nathan.
acceptable_variants: Nathan the prophet who told the parable of the ewe lamb
nathan confronted david with the parable of the poor man's lamb

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: Uriah's wife whom David took after seeing her bathing; David's adultery and arranging of Uriah's death is the great sin of his reign and leads to lasting consequences for his household
Nathan's story to David in 2 Samuel 12 about a rich man who seized a poor man's beloved lamb; designed to provoke David's judgment before revealing David was the rich man
